description: Stage 8c enhancement-design comment object. Structured output — the workflow's bash renderer turns this into the posted markdown. No free-form prose slots beyond `acknowledgment_line` and per-surface `text`; design questions are drawn from a fixed taxonomy by ID only.

acknowledgment_line.description: One sentence in hypothesis voice acknowledging the request without agreeing to implement it. Starts with 'Looks like', 'Likely', 'Appears to', or 'Worth checking first'. Example: 'Looks like the ask is to surface an in-app scheduler that survives window close.'

existing_surfaces.description: Existing code the enhancement would touch, with citations. Zero entries is valid — some enhancement requests don't map cleanly to existing surfaces, in which case the comment still carries design questions. Max three entries to keep the comment short.

text.description: One-line description of the surface in hypothesis voice. Example: 'app.on("window-all-closed") currently quits the app, which the minimize-to-tray request would need to intercept.'

scope_note.description: One or two sentences, hypothesis voice, set ONLY when the ask appears to fall in a class the repo's settled policy declines (patch-zero D-002: the official app.asar ships byte-identical; scope policy: net-new features not present upstream are default-declined). Names the policy so the maintainer's likely answer isn't a surprise. Null when policy doesn't obviously apply — a wrong policy note is worse than none.

design_question_ids.description: Keys into taxonomies/enhancement-design-questions.json. The renderer looks up the human-readable question text; an invalid ID cannot be emitted because the enum is schema-enforced. Pick up to three questions that the request actually raises — don't pad, and emit an empty array when none genuinely apply (non-app-code surfaces like CI scripts or repo ops, or a scope_note that already answers the ask).
